slots.meaning: The adult diver protagonist (a codename/handle, not necessarily a real name).
slots.guidance: A single capable, professional treasure-diver or explorer; never armed, never reckless for its own sake.

slots.meaning: The bare, reusable noun/adjective for the competing diver and their expedition (used as 'the RIVAL diver', 'a RIVAL buoy', 'RIVAL gear', 'the RIVAL's line', etc.).
slots.guidance: A competing explorer or team racing for the same prize; a rival in ambition and skill only, never a source of physical violence or a weapon threat.

slots.meaning: Capitalized form of RIVAL, used only in two setback ending titles ('The RIVAL_TITLE Buoy', 'Fouled on the RIVAL_TITLE Line').
slots.guidance: The same competing-explorer concept as RIVAL, title-cased; never a weapon or violent framing.

slots.meaning: The bare, pervasive short name for the whole sunken/ruined site being explored (used constantly as 'the COURT', 'the drowned COURT', and it closes several ending titles verbatim, e.g. 'The Court Sealed').
slots.guidance: A single common noun for the overall ruin/site; wonder over dread; must read as a place that can be safely entered, explored, and left.

slots.meaning: The bare collective noun for the three treasure pieces recovered over the course of the dive (used as 'the REGALIA', 'full REGALIA', 'a REGALIA piece'); it also closes several positive ending titles.
slots.guidance: A collective noun for a set of valuable, non-hazardous recovered items (treasure, relics, instruments); never itself dangerous to hold or carry.

slots.meaning: The first of the three regalia pieces, recovered at the THRONE position.
slots.guidance: A wearable ornamental treasure (a crown-like piece); a benign keepsake, never a weapon.

slots.meaning: A title-cased synonym for CIRCLET, used only in the setback ending title 'Walled from the CIRCLET_TITLE'.
slots.guidance: A synonym for the same wearable-treasure concept as CIRCLET (e.g. a crown/coronet word); never a weapon.

slots.meaning: The second of the three regalia pieces, recovered at the RELIQUARY position.
slots.guidance: A held ceremonial treasure object; a benign keepsake, never a weapon despite its shape.

slots.meaning: The third of the three regalia pieces, recovered at the CISTERN position.
slots.guidance: A small wearable treasure (a seal or signet piece); a benign keepsake, never a weapon.

slots.meaning: The bare title for the site's original, long-dead ruler, used as a possessive adjective ('the QUEEN's RING', 'the last QUEEN').
slots.guidance: A historical ruler or leader figure whose regalia is being recovered; a figure of the past, not a present threat.

slots.meaning: The single recurring condition that makes the dive/entry possible, named once at the outset.
slots.guidance: A survivable, recurring environmental window (a tide state, a weather lull, an access schedule); never itself dangerous.

slots.meaning: One of the four approach paths into the TIDEGATE at the very start.
slots.guidance: A passable, non-hazardous approach route; distinct from the other three.

slots.meaning: One of the four approach paths into the TIDEGATE at the very start.
slots.guidance: A passable, non-hazardous approach route; distinct from the other three.

slots.meaning: One of the four approach paths into the TIDEGATE at the very start (the RIVAL's own old approach).
slots.guidance: A passable, non-hazardous approach route; distinct from the other three.

slots.meaning: The fourth, trap-like approach path that always ends in an immediate setback ('Taken by the Undertow').
slots.guidance: A tempting-looking but genuinely unsafe-feeling shortcut; still non-lethal in outcome (the ending is an aborted dive, never death).

slots.meaning: The bare name for the first waypoint/hub of the site, just inside the entrance.
slots.guidance: A short name for a gated or mechanized entry feature; passable, not a death-trap.

slots.meaning: A short, ending-title form of TIDEGATE, used only in the setback ending title 'Pinned at the TIDEGATE_TITLE'.
slots.guidance: The same entry-feature concept as TIDEGATE, in its shortest ending-title form.

slots.meaning: The bare name for the second waypoint/hub of the site, where CIRCLET is recovered.
slots.guidance: A short name for a ceremonial hall or dais; passable, not a death-trap.

slots.meaning: A short, ending-title form of THRONE, used only in the setback ending title 'Outraced at the THRONE_TITLE'.
slots.guidance: The same ceremonial-hall concept as THRONE, in its shortest ending-title form.

slots.meaning: The bare name for the third waypoint/hub of the site, where SCEPTER is recovered.
slots.guidance: A short name for a vault or storehouse of relics; passable, not a death-trap.

slots.meaning: The bare name for the fourth waypoint/hub of the site, where RING is recovered.
slots.guidance: A short name for a large water-storage or reservoir chamber; passable, not a death-trap.

slots.meaning: The bare name for the fifth and final waypoint/hub of the site, where the RIVAL makes a last stand and the ascent begins.
slots.guidance: A short name for a domed or instrument-filled chamber with a way up to the surface; passable, not a death-trap.

slots.meaning: One of seven recurring filler-passage motifs used across the many dead-end side-branches; this one names a submerged, statue-lined hall.
slots.guidance: A benign, passable underwater passage feature; the branches that use it always end in a non-lethal setback, never death.

slots.meaning: One of seven recurring filler-passage motifs; this one names a narrow, sediment-choked crawlspace.
slots.guidance: A benign, passable tight passage feature; the branches that use it always end in a non-lethal setback, never death.

slots.meaning: One of seven recurring filler-passage motifs; this one names a long, ward-humming processional corridor.
slots.guidance: A benign, passable corridor feature; the branches that use it always end in a non-lethal setback, never death.

slots.meaning: One of seven recurring filler-passage motifs; this one names a descending stairwell.
slots.guidance: A benign, passable descending-stair feature; the branches that use it always end in a non-lethal setback, never death.

slots.meaning: One of seven recurring filler-passage motifs; this one names a partly-collapsed storage vault.
slots.guidance: A benign, passable vault/chamber feature; the branches that use it always end in a non-lethal setback, never death.

slots.meaning: One of seven recurring filler-passage motifs; this one names a strong current/surge feature.
slots.guidance: A benign-but-forceful current feature; the branches that use it always end in a non-lethal setback, never death.

slots.meaning: One of seven recurring filler-passage motifs; this one names a flooding, ceiling-failing hall.
slots.guidance: A benign-but-alarming flooding-hall feature; the branches that use it always end in a non-lethal setback, never death.
